subject: Ecology
title: Human-Assisted Dispersal
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Human-Assisted Dispersal.

hook.text: Humans are the greatest dispersal agents islands have ever known—and not always on purpose.

buildup.text: Polynesian voyagers carried pigs, chickens, rats, and crop plants across the Pacific.

discovery.text: Today, ships, planes, and cargo containers move species to islands at an unprecedented rate.

twist.text: Brown tree snakes reached Guam in cargo after World War II and wiped out most native birds.

climax.text: Biosecurity at ports and airports is now the front line of island conservation.

punchline.text: We move species faster than any wind or current.

quiz.question: How did brown tree snakes reach Guam?
options: In military cargo after World War II
By swimming from a nearby island
